# Site Configuration -- Practitioner SEO

<!-- This file is generated by the onboarding skill. Every field is documented
     with inline comments. Advanced users can edit directly. -->

```yaml
# ============================================================================
# SITE BASICS
# ============================================================================

site:
  domain: ""
  # Your domain without protocol. Example: benchcraftworkshop.com

  gsc_property: ""
  # Google Search Console property identifier.
  # Domain properties use: sc-domain:example.com
  # URL-prefix properties use: https://example.com
  # The GSC tools will fail if this is wrong. Verify in GSC settings.

  cms: ""
  # Options: wordpress, shopify, hubspot, squarespace, custom
  # Controls CMS-specific behavior in the optimizer and writer.

  seo_plugin: ""
  # WordPress only. Options: yoast, rankmath, seopress, none
  # Controls schema conflict detection and custom HTML block behavior.


# ============================================================================
# AUTHOR / ENTITY SETUP
# ============================================================================

author:
  name: ""
  # Full name as it should appear in schema markup and bylines.

  entity_id: ""
  # The @id for Person schema. Usually: https://yourdomain.com/#author
  # Must be consistent across all pages. If your site already has Person
  # schema, use the existing @id.

  credentials: ""
  # 1-2 sentences describing expertise. This is the E-E-A-T signal used in
  # schema (knowsAbout) and in credential prose on pages.
  # Example: "Professional furniture maker with 20 years of experience.
  # Author of The Unplugged Workshop and Joinery by Hand."

  profile_urls:
    # List all authoritative profile URLs. These become sameAs links in
    # Person schema and strengthen entity signals.
    linkedin: ""
    amazon_author: ""
    goodreads: ""
    wikipedia: ""       # Leave blank if none
    wikidata_qid: ""    # Leave blank if none
    # Add additional profiles below:
    # industry_directory: ""

  knowledge_panel: false
  # Does searching your name on Google show a Knowledge Panel?
  # true / false / unknown
  # If false: the skills will recommend entity-building actions.


# ============================================================================
# COMPETITIVE LANDSCAPE
# ============================================================================

competitive_landscape:
  structural_competitors:
    # 2-5 domains that consistently outrank you. The skills use these to
    # set realistic ceiling expectations and to identify where optimization
    # can vs. cannot overcome structural advantages.
    #
    # Format:
    # - domain: reddit.com
    #   dominates: "community consensus queries, 'best X' rankings"
    # - domain: competitor.com
    #   dominates: "product reviews, comparison pages"
    []

  forum_dominance: false
  # Does Reddit or a forum platform structurally dominate head terms in
  # your niche? true / false
  # If true: the skills will prioritize AIO citation and long-tail queries
  # over organic position 1 for head terms.

  forum_query_types: ""
  # If forum_dominance is true: which query types do forums dominate?
  # Example: "best X, reviews, recommendations, community opinions"


# ============================================================================
# CONTENT ARCHITECTURE
# ============================================================================

content:
  commercial_model: "none"
  # Options: affiliate, direct_sales, lead_gen, none
  # Controls whether the writer includes a commercial section and what
  # type of content it contains.

  commercial_section_placement: "bottom_third"
  # Options: bottom_third, contextual, none
  # bottom_third (default): commercial section in the last third of the page
  # contextual: commercial mentions woven into relevant sections
  # none: no commercial section even if commercial_model is set

  pillar_cluster_status: "developing"
  # Options: established, developing, none
  # Informs how aggressively the strategist recommends new supporting posts.

  cannibalization_risk: "medium"
  # Options: high, medium, low
  # high: multiple pages target near-identical queries (common on sites with
  #   20+ posts in a niche). The skills will default-check for cannibalization
  #   before recommending any new content.
  # medium: some overlap but pages mostly differentiated
  # low: each page targets a distinct query


# ============================================================================
# CONTENT SYSTEM FILE PATHS
# ============================================================================
# These paths tell the skills where to find your content system files.
# All paths are relative to the project root.

context:
  site_context_file: "SITE_CONTEXT.md"
  # The internal link catalog. Lists every page on the site with URL, title,
  # and link relationships. All three skills depend on this file.

  voice_guide_file: "VOICE_GUIDE.md"
  # Sentence-level patterns, tone registers, anti-patterns. Generated by
  # onboarding voice analysis.

  forbidden_words_file: "FORBIDDEN.md"
  # Words, phrases, and constructions that must never appear in content.

  formatting_rules_file: ""
  # Niche-specific formatting rules. Leave blank if none.
  # Example: "FORMATTING_RULES.md"

  commercial_list_file: ""
  # Affiliate URLs, product URLs, or service URLs for the commercial section.
  # Required if commercial_model is affiliate or direct_sales.
  # Example: "AFFILIATE_LIST.md" or "PRODUCT_LIST.md"


# ============================================================================
# CMS QUIRKS (auto-populated by onboarding based on CMS + plugin)
# ============================================================================

cms_quirks:
  schema_array_nesting: false
  # WordPress + Yoast: true. The SEO plugin nests custom JSON-LD arrays
  # inside its @graph structure, producing invalid schema. Fix: use separate
  # <script> tags, each containing a single object.

  inline_tag_concatenation: false
  # WordPress: true. <em> and other inline tags without surrounding spaces
  # cause text to run together in the visual editor. Fix: always include
  # spaces around inline-formatted terms.

  theme_product_schema: false
  # Shopify: true. Theme automatically injects Product schema. Custom schema
  # must not duplicate it.

  # Add additional CMS-specific quirks below if discovered during use.


# ============================================================================
# FRESHNESS THRESHOLDS (optional override)
# ============================================================================
# Default thresholds from SEO_METHODOLOGY.md:
#   fresh: < 90 days
#   monitor: 90-180 days
#   stale: 180-365 days
#   critical: > 365 days
#
# Override below if your niche has different update cadences.
# Example for a news site: fresh < 7 days, stale > 30 days

# freshness_thresholds:
#   fresh_days: 90
#   monitor_days: 180
#   stale_days: 365


# ============================================================================
# METHODOLOGY OVERRIDES (optional, not encouraged)
# ============================================================================
# The SEO methodology is opinionated by design. If you disagree with a
# specific claim, you can override it here with a note explaining why.
#
# Format:
# methodology_overrides:
#   - section: "freshness"
#     override: "Use 30-day fresh threshold for this news site"
#     rationale: "Daily publication cadence; 90-day window is too loose"

# methodology_overrides: []


# ============================================================================
# MCP TOOL AVAILABILITY (updated by readiness check)
# ============================================================================

tools:
  gsc_available: false
  serpapi_available: false
  pagespeed_available: false
  scraper_available: false
  # These are set to true when the corresponding API keys are configured
  # and the MCP server is running. The skills use these flags for graceful
  # degradation -- if a tool is unavailable, they fall back to alternatives
  # and note what's missing in output.
```
